Package de.uka.ilkd.key.util

Class CommandLine

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.util.CommandLine
public final class CommandLine extends Object
A small framework to handle command lines.

Command line options can be defined beforehand, the command line be parsed and set options be queried. It is possible to print a short usage message.

Note one can use -- to end the list of command line options. This is used if an argument bears a name of a command line option.

Typical usage

 CommandLine cl = new CommandLine();
 cl.addOption("-help", null, "Print usage");
 cl.addOption("-noArg", null, "Parameter w/o argument");
 cl.addOption("-argument", "argname", "Parameter w/ argument");

 // public static void main(String[] args) :
 cl.parse(args);

 if (cl.isSet(CMDLINE_HELP)) {
     cl.printUsage(System.out);
     System.exit(0);
 }

 if (cl.isSet("-noArg")) {
     // do something
 }

 // retrieve a set value
 String s = cl.getString("-argument", "default value if not set");

 // or retrieve a set integer value
 // this throws a CommandLineException if not formatted as an integer
 int intVal = cl.getInteger("-argument", 42);

 list = cl.getArguments();
 // handle remaining arguments

    Instantiates a new command line handling object.

  • Constructor Details

    • CommandLine

      public CommandLine()
      Instantiates a new command line handling object.

  • Method Details

    • addSubCommand

      public CommandLine addSubCommand(String name)
      Adds a new subcommand with the given name. To be able to configure this subcommand (e.g., by adding options to the subcommand), the method returns the newly created CommandLine.
      Parameters:
      name - The name of the subcommand name to add. Must not start with '--' and must not already be registered as a subcommand.
      Returns:
      the CommandLine of the newly created subcommand.

    • getSubCommand

      public CommandLine getSubCommand(String name)
      Returns the CommandLine for the given subcommand name if existing.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the subcommand
      Returns:
      the CommandLine for the subcommand with the given name or null

    • subCommandUsed

      public boolean subCommandUsed(String name)
      Check if a subcommand with the given name has been used.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the subcommand
      Returns:
      true iff the subcommand was the one given

    • addOption

      public void addOption(String image, String parameter, String description)
      Adds a command line option to this handler.
      Parameters:
      image - the image of the option (e.g. -help)
      parameter - simple description/name of the argument, null if there is no argument for this option (e.g. <file>, time, path, ...
      description - the description of the option

    • addText

      public void addText(String description, boolean identToDescriptionColumn)
      Adds an additional help text to be printed on the usage page. Calling this method has no influence of the parsing of command line arguments.
      Parameters:
      description - the text to be displayed
      identToDescriptionColumn - if true, the code is printed underneath the remaining descriptions, otherwise it has the full length.

    • addTextPart

      public void addTextPart(String command, String description, boolean identToDescriptionColumn)
      Add help text of a command which is not part of teh KeY prover, but part of the running script
      Parameters:
      command -
      description -
      identToDescriptionColumn -

    • addSection

      public void addSection(String text)
      Add Section Heading without any indentation
      Parameters:
      text -

    • parse

      public void parse(String[] args) throws CommandLineException
      Parses the command line.
      Parameters:
      args - typically the array of command line arguments passed to the main method.
      Throws:
      CommandLineException - If a option is unknown or badly formatted.

    • getArguments

      public List<String> getArguments()

    • getFileArguments

      public ArrayList<File> getFileArguments()
      Gets the non-option arguments of the command line.
      Returns:
      a read-only list of strings.

    • isSet

      public boolean isSet(String param)
      Checks if a boolean command line option is set.
      Parameters:
      param - the image of the command line option to be checked
      Returns:
      true if the option is set, false otherwise

    • getString

      public String getString(String param, String defaultValue)
      Gets the parameter string passed to an argument option. If the parameter has not been specified, return the default value.
      Parameters:
      param - the command line option. Needs to take an argument
      defaultValue - the default value to return if option is not set
      Returns:
      either the set option or defaultValue if not set.

    • getInteger

      public int getInteger(String param, int defaultValue) throws CommandLineException
      Gets the value of an integer command line option. If not present, a default value is returned. If the argument cannot be parsed as a (positive or negative) integer, a CommandLineException is thrown.
      Parameters:
      param - the option to retrieve
      defaultValue - the default value to use if no value specified
      Returns:
      either the set option or defaultValue if not set.
      Throws:
      CommandLineException - if the argument is ill-formatted.

    • getLong

      public long getLong(String param, long defaultValue) throws CommandLineException
      Gets the value of a long integer command line option. If not present, a default value is returned. If the argument cannot be parsed as a (positive or negative) integer, a CommandLineException is thrown.
      Parameters:
      param - the option to retrieve
      defaultValue - the default value to use if no value specified
      Returns:
      either the set option or defaultValue if not set.
      Throws:
      CommandLineException - if the argument is ill-formatted.

    • printUsage

      public void printUsage(PrintStream stream)
      Prints the usage page for this command line.

      Every of the page is indented to the level set by setIndentation(int) (default is 0). The options and help texts appear in the order of definition (by addOption(String, String, String) and addText(String, boolean)) The descriptions of the options All option descriptions commence in the same column.

      Descriptions which would result in lines longer than getLineLength() characters are broken at spaces (if that is possible).

      Parameters:
      stream - the stream to print to (typically System.out)

    • getIndentation

      public int getIndentation()
      Gets the indentation depth. This is the number of spaces which is put in front of the options when printing the help screen.
      Returns:
      a non-negative number

    • setIndentation

      public void setIndentation(int indentSize)
      Sets the indentation depth to the argument. This is the number of spaces which is put in front of the options when printing the usage page.
      Parameters:
      indentSize - a non-negative number

    • getLineLength

      public int getLineLength()
      Gets the line length used for the usage page. This is the number of characters printed in one line when printing the usage page. Longer lines are broken at spaces (if possible).
      Returns:
      the line length

    • setLineLength

      public void setLineLength(int lineLength)
      Sets the line length used for the usage page. This is the number of characters printed in one line when printing the usage page. Longer lines are broken at spaces (if possible).
